rulings.Case Title: IN RE THE JASMINE LIN IRREVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Court-appointed fiduciaries are required to supplement or amend their own petitions; Counsel should refrain from filing her own "declarations" that are in the form of answer sheets to issues raised by an examiner in a tentative ruling. It is also unnecessary to recite the particulars of all of the prior accountings already adjudicated. The only pertinent orders for these successor co-trustees were the 12/14/21 order that appointed them and the 7/26/22 order discharging the former trustee. Transfer of cash between accounts is NOT a change in the form of assets that needs to be reported on an informational schedule. [Probate Code Section 1063(b).] Status of closing the trust administration will be set for 4/9/24. A second account and petition to terminate the trust must be filed by 3/25/24. Appearance will be required on 4/9/24 if no petition is timely filed. (When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 2-1/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.] This includes proposed orders. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).])

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF LEONISA A. TRIAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/20/24. (No earlier availability.) This purportedly amended account is identical to the one filed on 11/30/22 with the exception of just a few issues being addressed. Other issues posted in the tentative ruling for the hearing on 1/17/23 have not been addressed: (1) The beginning bank statement is not for the period immediately PRECEDING the date the conservators were appointed. [Probate Code Sect. 2620(c)(2).] It is for the conservatorship's US Bank account, not the conservatee's. (2) A final conservatorship accounting must end on the DATE OF DEATH and also include a supplemental accounting following the date of death. [Probate Code Section 2620(b).] (3) What were the "additional assets received" from the Wells Fargo account? No Wells Fargo account was inventoried. There are also "miscellaneous" deposits on 10/19/21 and 12/16/21 without indicating the source. (4) The invoice for attorney costs attached as Exhibit E does not have adequate descriptions. The dates do not correspond to dates reflected on the Court's register of actions. What was delivered to the Court or filed and returned (see entries on 7/1/20, 11/4/20, three on 1/4/21, 3/24/20, and 4/7/20)? (5) There were credit card payments. Please file statements confirming the purchases and amounts paid. (6) There were life insurance premiums paid but no life insurance policy inventoried or on hand. (7) The Court Investigator reported that the conservatee purchased a double burial plot in 2003, but none was inventoried or accounted for. (8) The accounting for the period 7/15/21 - 9/14/22 shows a disbursements total on the summary that differs from the total shown on Schedule C-1. Courtesy copies of anything filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 2/7/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B. (After all issues are addressed, an appearance will likely be required for the Court to determine if attorney fees requested are reasonable. There are no statutory fees in conservatorships. It is not necessary to give grandchildren notice if their parents survived the deceased conservatee; the conservatee's children are her heirs. Only more remote issue would be heirs if they are the issue of a predeceased child or more remote issue.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GEMMA RUTH PAYBARAH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/20/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No courtesy copies have been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] (3) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (4) Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) requires notice if an HEIR or beneficiary has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. (The beneficiary of this estate is a trust. Heirs are not necessarily beneficiaries. [Probate Code Sections 24 and 44.] (5) No allowance or rejection of the creditorâs claim (or satisfaction of the claim) appears to have been filed. [Probate Code Section 9250(b).] Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies, including of any papers filed to address these issues, to the assigned examiner by 2/7/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.
